overallDetail: All seven acceptance legs pass, R1-R11 satisfied. The final leg — physical Android WebView boot — was run by the conductor per the plan's tail ownership and recorded under evidence/device/; every other leg passed off-device in the worker run.

identity-save-reopen-duplicate.detail: All scene-object UUIDs and semantic metadata survived editor save/close/reopen byte-stably; the editor duplicate received a fresh UUID in the same semantic parent with binding retained verbatim, then was explicitly retargeted to the second currency through the inspector; every observation is hash-bracketed in the session ledger and re-validated against committed bytes by tests.

live-copy-preview.detail: NOT native: inspector fields commit only on the change event (blur/Enter); three recorded keystrokes left the canvas byte-identical, the commit updated it. The smallest recorded plugin path (editor-plugins/live-copy-preview, loaded via the documented -plugins flag) forwards input events through the editor's own commit path: three keystrokes produced three distinct canvas states.

unsafe-cases-inert-or-blocked.detail: Hostile copy and a hostile component property (quote, double quote, backtick, ${}, {}, newline, */, //, </script>) round-trip byte-exact into plain string literals; the compiler escapes </script> as <\/script> (same runtime value); AST tests prove hostile content exists only inside string literals. Invalid catalog id, missing binding, missing/duplicate semantic id, and unknown texture block with named codes and zero writes.

deterministic-generation.detail: Two CompileProject runs from unchanged editor state — with generated outputs deleted between runs — produced byte-identical Probe.ts and Semantic.ts (recorded twice: pre-duplicate scene and final scene). No normalization needed; volatile-field registry is empty.

offline-runtime-build.detail: Clean sparse checkout of exact pushed repair commit fe3a8aae on the Ubuntu host, npm ci, then the full verifier inside a systemd unit restricted to AF_UNIX: a curl negative control proved internet sockets unavailable before all 7/7 steps passed. Phaser Editor was absent; the build uses generated scene code + phaser@4.2.1 only, and the bundle contains the probe sentinel with no editor-package markers.

android-webview-boot.detail: Conductor-run leg from the same clean fe3a8aae Ubuntu checkout: locked install/build, Capacitor Android generation, Gradle assemble, an exact-checkout Vite preview with its working directory verified, adb reverse tcp:8843, install, launch, and host screencap all passed on a physical Pixel 6a (Android 16, WebView 149.0.7827.164). boot.png is content-only (Phaser canvas rendering PROBE-43QVBIH7, SAFE-TOP marker, sprites, hostile-string footer; no system bars, notifications, or identifiers). The scrubbed logcat records only localhost load/start and the Phaser v4.2.1 WebGL banner with no context failure; device-boot.json carries commit/model/OS/WebView only, no serial.

dependency-enumeration.detail: Complete enumeration in the dependencies section: exact runtime/dev pins with lockfile-resolved versions, machine tools, editor artifacts with commit-worthiness, lockfile implications for the goal-U1 freeze, and the plugin verdict.

ladder.detail: Scene JSON natively carries a stable per-object UUID (id) plus label, which survive save/reopen/duplicate — but there is no native field for role/binding/slot/variant, so built-ins cannot hold the full semantic contract.

A Semantic user component (fabSemanticId/fabRole/fabBinding/fabSlot/fabVariant string props) serializes into the scene JSON as flat 'Semantic.<prop>' keys, survives save/reopen/duplicate, is editable in the inspector, and compiles into runtime code as plain property assignments on a generated Semantic class (imports phaser only).

headless_regen.detail: The scene compiler runs only inside the workbench browser client (product.all plugin). The server binary exposes a JSON API (POST /editor/api) for files/state but no compile command; the desktop MCP bridge (port 1902) forwards tool calls to a RUNNING GUI workbench; no CLI compile surface exists. Mitigations observed and recorded: the editor auto-compiles on save (scene save and generated output move together), and the full workbench is scriptable against the local server via HTTP + browser automation — that is automation of the GUI, not headless support, and is recorded as a vendor/automation cost for goal-U7.

normalization.detail: Both recorded double-generations were byte-identical, so the volatile-field registry is empty and normalization is the identity function. Any future entry must arrive together with evidence per KTD8.

confinement.ktd11: Worktree-safe. A stat manifest of the entire enclosing worktree outside the fixture (gitignored files included; .git/node_modules excluded) was identical before and after all editor sessions. The editor accepted the long dotted .twf-worktrees path. No duplicate-checkout environment is needed for the Phaser lane.

live_preview.detail: Base editor: form fields (createStringField/createStringDialogField) commit exclusively on the native change event — no per-keystroke update; recorded with per-keystroke canvas hashes. Plugin path: a 26-line plugin loaded via the documented -plugins flag forwards input events on formText fields into the same commit path, giving true per-keystroke preview at the cost of one undo step per keystroke.

validate.detail: Scriptable, deterministic, editor-free: publish-check.mjs parses editor-native scene JSON, catalog, and asset pack and returns named block codes.
validate.outcomeMapping: clean result is publishable; blocked-invalid-catalog-id / blocked-invalid-binding / blocked-missing-semantic-id / blocked-duplicate-semantic-id / blocked-unknown-texture map onto unsupported-intent and invalid-revision shapes in the shared typed vocabulary.

publish.detail: Editor-native sources plus committed editor-generated code are plain files; hashing them into an immutable revision is scriptable without editor or account. Caveat carried from headless_regen: generated code cannot be independently regenerated headlessly, so publish pins committed generated bytes by hash and relies on the editor's auto-compile-on-save pairing.
publish.outcomeMapping: success maps to applied; unchanged input maps to no-op (deterministic generation proven byte-identical).

preflight.detail: Validate plus hash comparison of scene/generated/catalog/assets in plain node.
preflight.outcomeMapping: hash mismatch maps to blocked-drift; unknown or incomplete revision maps to invalid-revision.

apply.detail: Applying an accepted revision is immutable file copy plus an atomic pointer flip; no editor involvement at apply time.
apply.outcomeMapping: applied / no-op / blocked-drift all derivable from content hashes.

status.detail: Reading the pointer and recomputing hashes is plain node.
status.outcomeMapping: reports the applied revision plus drift state in the typed vocabulary.

proof.detail: Local proof (offline build + bundle sentinel scan) is scriptable and runs inside verify. Physical-device proof needs the Android lane; that is an environmental dependency, not a toolchain capability gap.
proof.outcomeMapping: local proof green/failed; the device leg stays three-state per its own evidence.

license.product: Phaser Editor v5.0.2 desktop (Phaser Studio Inc), release date 2026-05-14
license.bundledPackageLicense: MIT declared in the desktop app's bundled package.json
license.unlock: The editor requires a phaser.io account login to unlock; the local install was pre-authenticated by the human before this card. The probe observed GetServerMode returning desktop:true unlocked:true without reading or exporting any credential.
license.authMaterialLocation: user home configuration (never read, never committed, not in evidence)

dev.playwright-core: ^1.58.2 (resolved 1.58.2; GUI-evidence tooling only — verify never uses it)

tools: Phaser Editor 5.0.2 desktop app — machine-local, human-authenticated; needed only to author scenes and (re)generate code; never needed to build, verify, or run
Chromium (playwright browser cache) — GUI-evidence sessions only

Node 26.3.0 / npm 11.16.0 on macOS for the editor sessions; Node 25.0.0 / npm 11.6.2 on Linux for the independent clean-checkout offline and device proof; verify is plain node with no version-specific APIs

editorArtifacts.kind: committed derived output — editor-generated, hash-pinned, regenerable only via the editor GUI
committed probe plugin, loaded via the documented -plugins flag; not required for identity or publication

dependencies.lockfileImplications: The fixture is a standalone package with its own package-lock.json; root workspaces exclude experiments/** so nothing here can touch the root manifest or lockfile. For the goal-U1 freeze, the Phaser lane needs phaser@4.2.1 (exact) plus the toolchain above added to the frozen workspace state (tools/phaser-shell), or a standalone-package layout like this fixture. No editor package ships to the runtime: generated code imports phaser only.
dependencies.pluginVerdict: Identity, roles, bindings, slots, and variants need NO plugin — editor-supported user components suffice (rung b of the ladder). The only plugin justified is the smallest live-copy-preview forwarder for per-keystroke preview, because the base editor commits fields only on blur/Enter; it is probe-scoped, 26 lines, and loaded through the supported -plugins extension point.

ledger.endingShaNote: db400d05 is the worker implementation commit. GUI report/evidence followed in 8a8467ca and a99fba6a; initial conductor Android proof is f3f05a55; validated review repairs are fe3a8aae. The accepted clean Ubuntu offline/device proof was reproduced from fe3a8aae and finalized in the following evidence commit.
ledger.agent: claude/fable worker for implementation and multi-lens review; Codex conductor for validated repairs, clean Ubuntu reproduction, and Android evidence; card 43Qvbih7
rework: scene-editor duplicate was a silent no-op under automation until clipboard-read/write permissions were granted to the browser context (duplicate is clipboard-backed copy+paste)
inspector user-component fields are textareas, not inputs — the first retarget attempts searched inputs only
playwright-core browser revision mismatch on this machine: the matching Chromium was installed into the user cache and the full build selected via an env override

systemd IPAddressDeny=any failed its curl negative control on the Ubuntu user manager, so that run was rejected; RestrictAddressFamilies=AF_UNIX then demonstrably blocked the network before verification

the first refreshed Android launch reached a stale older preview on port 8843, so its screenshot was rejected; the stale process was stopped and the accepted capture verified the preview working directory at fe3a8aae
humanInterventions: none during execution; precondition: the Phaser Editor install was already human-authenticated before the card started
addedDependencies: standalone fixture package only (see dependencies); no root manifest or lockfile change
ledger.changedSurface: experiments/design-frontends/fixtures/phaser-feasibility/** (the plan document landed earlier in the Planned stage)
failedGates: android-webview-boot: blocked during the worker run because no device lane was reachable; resolved by the conductor proof in f3f05a55 under acceptance.android-webview-boot and evidence/device/
